2606.16806.title: LLM-based Visual Code Completion for Aerospace Geometric Design
2606.16806.paper_summary: Recent advances in both Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision Language Models (VLMs) have seen a step change in their ability to perform visual code completion, but the aerospace industry, which prioritizes safety and explainabilty over rapid LLM adoption, currently has no publicly announced LLM-based geometric design copilot systems in commercial use by aerospace Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs). This paper presents a LLM-based visual programming copilot application for aerospace engineering design tasks, using a visual programming variant of the ReAct methodology and GPT 5.4. In addition to the copilot, we describe Wingbuilder, a new Grasshopper plugin library with custom components for aerospace-specific geometry abstraction, and an associated Aerospace Visual Programming Dataset (AVPD) with 18 aerospace expert designed tasks at different levels of difficulty alongside ground truth solutions. We evaluate our copilot application with a user trial involving two experienced aerospace engineers from a large aircraft manufacturing company. We find our copilot visual programming ReAct methodology was successful in generating suggestions that participants found helpful, but slow ReAct inference times limit its usefulness to more complex time-consuming tasks where waiting for good copilot solution suggestion was worthwhile. Participants reported they liked the tool and would be willing to use it in the future.
2606.16806.paper_summary_zh: 最近在大型語言模型（LLMs）和視覺語言模型（VLMs）方面的進展，使得它們在執行視覺代碼補全方面的能力有了顯著提升，但航空航天產業優先考慮安全性和可解釋性，而非快速採用LLM，目前尚未有航空航天原始設備製造商（OEM）公開宣布基於LLM的幾何設計副駕駛系統在商業上使用。本文提出了一種基於LLM的視覺編程副駕駛應用，專為航空工程設計任務而設，使用了ReAct方法論的視覺編程變體和GPT 5.4。除了副駕駛外，我們還描述了Wingbuilder，一個新的Grasshopper插件庫，具有針對航空航天特定幾何抽象的自定義組件，以及一個相關的航空航天視覺編程數據集（AVPD），其中包含18個由航空航天專家設計的不同難度級別的任務及其真實解決方案。我們通過一項用戶試驗評估了我們的副駕駛應用，該試驗涉及來自一家大型飛機製造公司的兩位經驗豐富的航空工程師。我們發現我們的副駕駛視覺編程ReAct方法論在生成參與者覺得有幫助的建議方面是成功的，但慢速的ReAct推理時間限制了其在更複雜且耗時任務中的實用性，因為等待良好的副駕駛解決方案建議是值得的。參與者報告他們喜歡這個工具，並願意在未來使用它。
2606.16806.author: Hau Kit Yong et.al.
2606.16806.authors: Hau Kit Yong, Robert Marsh, Edmar A. Silva, András Sóbester, Stuart E. Middleton
